mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which raises corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during months with positive PAYEMS changes over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.25 percent, the restrictive cost of capital exceeds the estimated neutral rate, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS > 2.25% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and declines in industrial production (INDPRO) signal stagflationary margin compression, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and falling INDPRO does not produce negative average 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE rising more than 0.3 percentage points over two months) signals an impending demand contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances during late-cycle periods, or the signal's Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) reflects contemporaneous consumer spending strength that immediately boosts corporate revenue surprises, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward returns following positive monthly RSAFS growth are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1.2 million units annualized signals deteriorating residential investment and backward linkages to construction and durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when HOUST < 1200 over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is increasing while consumer price inflation is decelerating, real policy rates rise faster than expected, tightening financial conditions abruptly and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising FEDFUNDS and falling CPIAUCSL does not produce negative average 5-day returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contracting month-over-month indicates a negative supply-demand loop where falling output precedes inventory corrections and job cuts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months following month-over-month INDPRO declines over a 3-year rolling window.
